evidence: The amber probe and periwinkle candidate dial form the clear central focus, with the lilac score bars playing a supporting readout role below.
The frame presents one cohesive system (probe, candidate arrows, score bars) rather than five-plus unrelated novel ideas.
Every candidate is labeled c1..c4 at its arrow tip and above its bar, and the probe is labeled on the arrow; there is no legend or key.
The only prose is the one-line instruction 'Turn the probe. Each bar tracks how closely it points at that candidate,' which annotates the visible dial directly above it.
The header, degree readouts, bar labels, and mono numeric scores are all legible including at the 375px width.
All elements including the score bars and the down-left probe in the anti-aligned frame sit inside the frame with margin; no term is cut by an edge.
In the aligned and anti-aligned frames the amber probe label collides with its own arrowhead, hiding part of the text so it reads 'probe ' with the degree obscured and 'be 200°' with 'pro' covered.
suggestion: Offset the probe's angle label radially outward from the arrowhead so the full 'probe NNN°' text never sits under the arrow tip at any orientation.

evidence: The probe wears amber (attention), candidate arrows periwinkle (observed), and score bars lilac (parameters), each concept holding one consistent color.
Every element — probe, candidate arrows, unit circle, and score bars with readouts — carries propositional content; no decoration is present.

Each driven state matches its promise: initial probe at 90° with symmetric 0.34/0.94/0.94/0.34, aligned c1 at 1.00, orthogonal c3 at 1.00 with c1 at 0.00, and anti-aligned c1 at -1.00 with its bar below the baseline.
Each candidate's arrow, bar, and numeric readout share the same c1..c4 label so the covariation is traceable without cross-frame hunting.
not applicable: frames are independent driven states and viewport widths, not a sequential morph build.
The dial and bar cluster are centered with generous margins and a clear top-to-bottom reading order at all three widths.
